intent: Same source domain (logrotate_t) denied on TWO DIFFERENT target types: var_log_t:file read and var_t:file write. Tests f4 contract #5 - perms are unioned WITHIN a (sdomain, ttype, tclass) triple but NEVER across triples. A naive grouper keyed only on sdomain (or sdomain+tclass, ignoring ttype) would wrongly union read+write into one rule spanning both types.
_kinds_note: Both denials are TeAllowable. NOTE: audit2why reports the var_log_t:file read as 'would be allowed by active policy' (logrotate legitimately reads logs in shipped policy) - the AVC text is still a real-kernel-valid INPUT record (a captured AVC does not retroactively change because policy would now allow it). The var_t:file write is a genuine TE gap. Both are valid parser/grouper inputs. floor_label none (same role, same level s0 on both).

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow logrotate_t var_log_t:file read; allow logrotate_t var_t:file write; (TWO separate lines - audit2allow keys its merge on the (type,type,class) triple, so it does NOT union read and write across the two different target types)
oracle.audit2allow_R: files_rw_var_files(logrotate_t) + logging_read_generic_logs(logrotate_t) (TWO different interface macros, each broad: files_rw_var_files grants rw on ALL var_t files, logging_read_generic_logs grants read on all generic log types)
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: -R substitutes two broad interfaces (files_rw_var_files grants WRITE+read+more on all var_t; logging_read_generic_logs grants read on the whole logging type set). Our expected.te keeps two raw allows on SEPARATE lines: var_log_t:file read and var_t:file write, perms NOT crossed. var_t gets only write (not rw), var_log_t gets only read.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A grouper keyed on sdomain alone (or sdomain+tclass, dropping ttype) would union the perms into a single rule like `allow logrotate_t { var_log_t var_t }:file { read write };` - granting WRITE to var_log_t and READ to var_t, neither of which was denied. The (sdomain, ttype, tclass) triple MUST be the full grouping key; perms union only within a triple. audit2allow -N gets this right; a hand-rolled grouper might not.
